model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: UConn is a top-tier program with a significant talent and efficiency advantage over Furman, which ranks outside the top 190 in both KenPom and Torvik metrics. While Furman has a credible upset path through disciplined play and three-point shooting, UConn's elite defense, physical frontcourt, and tournament experience make them the overwhelming favorite. The Huskies' ability to control the tempo and dominate the paint should neutralize Furman's strengths and secure a comfortable win.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Efficiency Analysis
reasoningStep.summary: UConn holds a decisive edge in advanced metrics and overall team quality compared to Furman.
reasoningStep.evidence: Furman ranks 191st in KenPom and 195th in Torvik, with an adjusted offensive efficiency of 107.5 and defensive efficiency of 109.5. UConn, while lacking specific ratings in the tool response, is consistently a top-10 program in recent years and is described in previews as a No. 2 seed with a strong frontcourt and physical defense.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

Boston College Eagles 2d Jeff Borzello Joe Lunardi's ultimate guide to men's March Madness 2026 Illustration by ESPN Joe Lunardi Mar 17, 2026, 09:45 AM ET Close Resident college basketball bracketologist for ESPN Contributor to SportsCenter, ESPN Insider Published first public bracket in 1995 Follow on X Multiple Authors Email Print Open Extended Reactions Our men's NCAA tournament preview has evolved since the first was created 30-odd years ago. It's how Bracketology began, when a handful of hoop nerds gathered during "Selection Weekend" to put together an 80-page postseason "Blue Ribbon" guide. It was the ultimate conclusion to spending the college basketball season poring over résumés, watching games and evaluating strengths, weaknesses and possibilities. All on the crew's own time. The guide has shrunk a tad in length and is no longer in print, but it still fulfills the same role: outlining the crucial information you need to fill out brackets. We've made the case for exactly how each team will advance -- and how they won't. Bracketologist in Chief Joe Lunardi also ran the numbers one last time to forecast where each will actually finish in the final field of 68. The results might or might not surprise you -- and could help you fill out those brackets . Jump to region: EAST |

result.text: Furman vs UConn prediction, pick, odds March Madness first round Skip to main content Home PGA Tour LPGA College Amateur Golf Life Equipment Apparel Newsletter Videos Events Odds Betting USA TODAY Sports Contact Us Furman basketball vs UConn prediction, our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Zachary Huber Greenville News Show Caption Hide Caption Watch coach Bob Richey react to Furman basketball's March Madness bid Furman men's basketball coach Bob Richey speaks at the team's Selection Sunday watch party. "We'll see you at Philly!" The Furman Paladins will face the UConn Huskies in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. Furman, a No. 15 seed, secured its spot by winning the Southern Conference tournament. The Paladins will need to counter UConn's strong frontcourt and physical defense to have a chance at an upset. Key matchups include Furman's Cooper Bowser against UConn's frontcourt and guard Alex Wilkins versus defender Silas Demary Jr. The Furman Paladins men's basketball team will face UConn in the opening round of the NCAA Tournament. The Paladins (22-12) were given the No. 15 seed and will attempt to upset No. 2 UConn (29-5) on March 20 at 10 p.m. on TBS . Furman secured its spot in 2026 March Madness after knocking off top-seeded East Tennessee State in the Southern Conference tournament final as a No. 6 seed. SLING TV: Watch Furman vs. UConn on Sling TV Furman overloaded the Buccaneers with its 3-point shooting, knocking down 10 of its 26 attempts. Asa Thomas, Alex Wilkins, Charles Johnston and Tom House all knocked down a pair of threes. Forward Cooper Bowser also took over in the paint, scoring 21 points and grabbing 11 rebounds to secure his first double-double of the season. In 2023, No. 13 Furman upset No. 4 Virginia, thanks to a late 3-pointer from guard JP Pegues. Here is what to know about the Huskies and our prediction for Furman's first-round game. Furman will have its hands full with UConn frontcourt The Huskies are led by center Tarris Reed Jr. and forward Alex Karaban. Reed Jr. leads UConn in scoring and rebounding at 13.7 points and 8.1 rebounds per game. Karaban averages 12 points per game and has made several clutch shots during their national championship runs in 2023 and 2024. Forwards Cooper Bowser, Charles Johnston and Ben Vander Wal will be tasked with slowing down UConn's top front-court players. For the Paladins to have a chance at the upset, it's imperative that Bowser, Johnson and Vander Wal stay out of foul trouble. Furman's Alex Wilkins vs UConn's Silas Demary Jr key matchup to watch Wilkins has had a phenomenal freshman season, averaging 17.7 points and 4.7 assists per game. He poured in 34 points and five assists in the Paladins' Southern Conference semifinal win against UNC Greensboro. Guard Silas Demary Jr. is considered UConn's top defender. He averages 1.7 steals per game and is a top-15 finalist for the Naismith Defensive Player of the Year award. Demary has helped coach Dan Hurley's team be the 11th

Build your feed Originals Watch More Sports Favorites Submit a story Sign out About Advertise Submit Story Contact Us Terms of Service Privacy Policy © 2026 BVM Sports. Best Version Media, LLC. Home Originals Watch More Sports Favorites Account Submit a story Sign out Search Your Des Moines Customers are Here. Where are You? Join our advertising network and connect directly with your local market! Furman vs. UConn NCAA Tournament First Round Preview: Key Matchup By BVM Sportsdesk, 03/16/2026 The No. 15 seed Furman Paladins will compete against the No. 2 seed UConn in the first round of the 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Championship on Friday in Philadelphia. This matchup comes after Furman's 76-61 win over ETSU, securing their eighth tournament appearance. Freshman Alex Wilkins, a standout player in the Southern Conference Tournament, led the team to this achievement. The winner will advance to face the winner of the game between No. 7 seed UCLA and No. 10 seed UCF. Full details on the tip-off time and broadcast will be released later. By the Numbers Furman (22-12) has won eight NCAA Tournament bids overall. Alex Wilkins scored a total of 65 points over three games in the Southern Conference Tournament, with a high of 34 points in the semifinals. State of Play Furman is making its second NCAA Tournament appearance in four years. The team secured its spot by defeating three teams in the SoCon Tournament, including a significant victory over the No. 1 seed. What's Next If Furman defeats UConn, they will advance to face the winner of the UCLA vs. UCF match-up in the second round on Sunday. Bottom Line Furman is a dark horse in this year's tournament, armed with momentum from their recent SoCon victory and a talented roster, making them a team to watch in the opening rounds. A fan? modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.text: Furman vs UConn prediction, pick, odds March Madness first round Skip to main content Home PGA Tour LPGA College Amateur Golf Life Equipment Apparel Newsletter Videos Events Odds Betting USA TODAY Sports Contact Us Furman basketball vs UConn prediction, our pick for 2026 NCAA bracket first round Zachary Huber Greenville News Show Caption Hide Caption Watch coach Bob Richey react to Furman basketball's March Madness bid Furman men's basketball coach Bob Richey speaks at the team's Selection Sunday watch party. "We'll see you at Philly!" The Furman Paladins will face the UConn Huskies in the first round of the NCAA Tournament. Furman, a No. 15 seed, secured its spot by winning the Southern Conference tournament. The Paladins will need to counter UConn's strong frontcourt and physical defense to have a chance at an upset. Key matchups include Furman's Cooper Bowser against UConn's frontcourt and guard Alex Wilkins versus defender Silas Demary Jr. The Furman Paladins men's basketball team will face UConn in the opening round of the NCAA Tournament. The Paladins (22-12) were given the No. 15 seed and will attempt to upset No. 2 UConn (29-5) on March 20 at 10 p.m. on TBS . Furman secured its spot in 2026 March Madness after knocking off top-seeded East Tennessee State in the Southern Conference tournament final as a No. 6 seed. SLING TV: Watch Furman vs. UConn on Sling TV Furman overloaded the Buccaneers with its 3-point shooting, knocking down 10 of its 26 attempts. Asa Thomas, Alex Wilkins, Charles Johnston and Tom House all knocked down a pair of threes. Forward Cooper Bowser also took over in the paint, scoring 21 points and grabbing 11 rebounds to secure his first double-double of the season. In 2023, No. 13 Furman upset No. 4 Virginia, thanks to a late 3-pointer from guard JP Pegues. Here is what to know about the Huskies and our prediction for Furman's first-round game. Furman will have its hands full with UConn frontcourt The Huskies are led by center Tarris Reed Jr. and forward Alex Karaban. Reed Jr. leads UConn in scoring and rebounding at 13.7 points and 8.1 rebounds per game. Karaban averages 12 points per game and has made several clutch shots during their national championship runs in 2023 and 2024. Forwards Cooper Bowser, Charles Johnston and Ben Vander Wal will be tasked with slowing down UConn's top front-court players. For the Paladins to have a chance at the upset, it's imperative that Bowser, Johnson and Vander Wal stay out of foul trouble. Furman's Alex Wilkins vs UConn's Silas Demary Jr key matchup to watch Wilkins has had a phenomenal freshman season, averaging 17.7 points and 4.7 assists per game. He poured in 34 points and five assists in the Paladins' Southern Conference semifinal win against UNC Greensboro. Guard Silas Demary Jr. is considered UConn's top defender. He averages 1.7 steals per game and is a top-15 finalist for the Naismith Defensive Player of the Year award. Demary has helped coach Dan Hurley's team be the 11th

Furman vs. UConn NCAA Tournament First Round Preview: Key Matchup By BVM Sportsdesk, 03/16/2026 The No. 15 seed Furman Paladins will compete against the No. 2 seed UConn in the first round of the 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Championship on Friday in Philadelphia. This matchup comes after Furman's 76-61 win over ETSU, securing their eighth tournament appearance. Freshman Alex Wilkins, a standout player in the Southern Conference Tournament, led the team to this achievement. The winner will advance to face the winner of the game between No. 7 seed UCLA and No. 10 seed UCF. Full details on the tip-off time and broadcast will be released later. By the Numbers Furman (22-12) has won eight NCAA Tournament bids overall. Alex Wilkins scored a total of 65 points over three games in the Southern Conference Tournament, with a high of 34 points in the semifinals. State of Play Furman is making its second NCAA Tournament appearance in four years. The team secured its spot by defeating three teams in the SoCon Tournament, including a significant victory over the No. 1 seed. What's Next If Furman defeats UConn, they will advance to face the winner of the UCLA vs. UCF match-up in the second round on Sunday. Bottom Line Furman is a dark horse in this year's tournament, armed with momentum from their recent SoCon victory and a talented roster, making them a team to watch in the opening rounds. A fan?
